data.notes: The gate is false-positive-matching benign stdout, and the worker can fix it from their side. Every 'new failure' line is the tickmarkr CLI summary ('# done, # failed, ...') that approve.test.ts lets leak to stdout — the baseline gate's failure-line fingerprint sees the token 'failed' and counts it as a new failure, even though vitest reports the tests green (three consecutive green worker runs across two vendors, same gate output each time). Instructions for retry: in tests/cli/approve.test.ts, stop the command output reaching stdout — capture/mock console.log (or assert on approve()'s returned string, which is tickmarkr's convention: commands return the report, the CLI prints it once) so no run-summary lines are emitted during npm test. Do NOT weaken the gate or edit .tickmarkr/config.yaml; also revert the taskTimeoutMinutes overlay edit a prior attempt made — operator config is out of scope. Separately: file a tickmarkr defect that the baseline gate fingerprints stdout noise containing 'failed' as test failures; that's a real harness bug worth its own task.
